topics.title: Medicare 2026 Fee Schedule Revenue Impact Calculator
topics.angle: Enter your top CPT codes with annual volumes and 2025 payment; apply the 2026 conversion factors and efficiency/practice-expense adjustments to compute the per-code and total revenue delta, split by facility vs office site of service, so a practice sees exactly where the 2026 rule helps or hurts.
topics.rationale: No healthcare reimbursement model in the library; distinct from the 07-20 payer-underpayment analyzer (contracted vs paid) and break-even capacity model. Fresh with the CY2026 MPFS final rule. Pairs with the 2026 fee-schedule blog.

topics.title: 13-Week Rolling Cash Flow Forecast with Scenarios
topics.angle: A true weekly 13-week direct cash forecast: opening cash, receipts by customer, disbursements by category, and closing cash per week, with a base/upside/downside scenario toggle and an actual-vs-forecast variance column that rolls the window forward each week.
topics.rationale: Existing cash templates are bi-weekly, monthly period-end, and formal direct/indirect statements; none is a weekly 13-week rolling model with scenarios. On-site blog says QBO cannot do this, so the downloadable fills a real gap. Distinct from all prior template runs.

topics.title: Quarterly Estimated Tax Calculator (Self-Employed) 2026
topics.angle: From projected net profit and prior-year tax, compute each 2026 quarterly payment using the safe-harbor rule (90% current / 100% or 110% prior), add self-employment tax at 15.3%, and produce the four voucher amounts and due dates with an underpayment-penalty check.
topics.rationale: No estimated-tax calculator exists; the 07-20 tip/overtime estimator and 1099 vendor tracker are different. Pairs with the estimated-taxes blog. Dated for the 2026 payment cycle.

topics.title: Construction Change Order Log and Margin Impact Tracker
topics.angle: Log every change order against the original contract: description, approved cost, approved price, status, and date; the model rolls up added revenue vs added cost, flags work performed but unpriced, and shows the running effect on contract margin and value.
topics.rationale: Existing construction files are WIP schedule, job cost analysis, project budget, cash flow planner, and equipment cost; no change-order log. Distinct from the 07-19 WIP and job-cost models. Pairs with the change-order blog.

topics.title: Restaurant Break-Even and Daily Sales Target Calculator
topics.angle: From fixed monthly costs, prime-cost percentage, and other variable costs, compute the monthly break-even sales and translate it into a daily and weekly sales target and covers-needed number, with a profit-target mode that shows the sales required to hit a chosen margin.
topics.rationale: The existing break-even templates are ecommerce and indifference-point (generic); no restaurant-specific break-even. Distinct from the 07-20 labor optimizer and recipe cost calculator. Ties operating costs to a daily number operators can act on.

topics.title: Client Profitability and Realization Tracker
topics.angle: For each client: annual fee, budgeted vs actual hours by staff level, standard rate, realization and effective rate, write-offs, and a margin flag, so a firm can see which clients are unprofitable and where scope creep or under-pricing is eating capacity.
topics.rationale: No client-profitability model in existing files. Distinct from the multi-client close tracker (close status) and the 07-20 busy-season capacity planner (staffing hours). Targets the price-advisory and margin pain.

topics.title: Provider Productivity (wRVU) and Compensation Tracker
topics.angle: Track each provider's work RVUs against target, compensation per wRVU, and productivity variance by month, with a group rollup, so a practice can see who is above or below target and tie compensation to output under the shifting 2026 RVU values.
topics.rationale: No provider-productivity model exists; distinct from the 07-20 break-even capacity model (cost per visit) and the fee-schedule impact calculator (payment rates). Fresh given the 2026 work-RVU efficiency adjustment.

topics.title: Owner Pay and Tax Set-Aside Planner
topics.angle: From monthly profit, prescribe a sustainable owner draw, a tax-reserve percentage moved to a separate account each month, and the operating buffer to keep, so an owner pays themselves without starving the business or the January tax bill.
topics.keyword: how much should I set aside for taxes small business
topics.rationale: No owner-pay/tax-reserve model exists. Distinct from the 07-20 profit-to-cash reconciliation (explains the gap) and the estimated-tax calculator (computes the IRS payment); this prescribes the monthly draw-and-reserve routine.

topics.title: 1099-K Reconciliation Worksheet 2026
topics.angle: Reconcile a received 1099-K's gross amount to the books: back out refunds, sales tax, tips, personal transfers, and chargebacks to arrive at reportable revenue, with a per-processor breakdown and a variance flag, sized for the 2026 $20,000/200-transaction reset.
topics.rationale: Distinct from the 07-20 1099/W-9 vendor tracker (issuer side, forms you send); this is the recipient side, reconciling a 1099-K you received. No such template exists. Fresh for the 2026 threshold reset.

topics.title: Construction Retainage Tracker and Release Schedule
topics.angle: Per project and per billing period, track retainage withheld (5-10%), the balance held, expected release dates, and aging of overdue releases, with a portfolio rollup of total cash tied up in retainage, so contractors chase releases and forecast the cash instead of losing track of it.
topics.rationale: No retainage model in existing files. Distinct from the WIP schedule and the change-order log; retainage is a separate balance-sheet line and cash-flow driver. Targets the retainage cash-flow pain directly.
